questions.q: Dr. Rajkumar was a towering star of which Indian film industry?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar was the most celebrated star of Kannada cinema across the second half of the 20th century.

questions.q: In which 1954 film did Dr. Rajkumar make his debut as a lead actor?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar debuted as a lead in the 1954 Kannada film 'Bedara Kannappa'.

questions.q: In which year was the Kannada actor Dr. Rajkumar born?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar was born in 1929.

questions.q: In which year did the Kannada legend Dr. Rajkumar pass away?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar died in 2006.

questions.q: Apart from acting, in which artistic discipline was Dr. Rajkumar formally trained?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar was a trained Carnatic singer who also sang playback for many of his films.

questions.q: Which of India's top film honours was awarded to Dr. Rajkumar in 1995?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest film honour, in 1995.

questions.q: Approximately how many Kannada films did Dr. Rajkumar act in over his career?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar acted in around 200 Kannada films across five decades.

questions.q: For which craft did Dr. Rajkumar win a National Film Award in 1992?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar won the National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer in 1992.

questions.q: Over how many decades did Dr. Rajkumar's acting career span?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar's film career spanned roughly five decades, from 1954 onward.

questions.q: By which respectful title were fans most fond of calling the Kannada icon Dr. Rajkumar?
questions.explanation: Kannada audiences affectionately called Dr. Rajkumar 'Annavru', meaning elder brother.

questions.q: What was the birth name of Dr. Rajkumar before he became a film star?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar was born Singanalluru Puttaswamayya Muthuraju.

questions.q: In the 1954 debut film 'Bedara Kannappa', Dr. Rajkumar played a devotee of which deity?
questions.explanation: In 'Bedara Kannappa' he played Kannappa, a hunter and ardent devotee of Lord Shiva.

questions.q: Which 1972 blockbuster, a landmark of Kannada cinema, starred Dr. Rajkumar in a much-celebrated role?
questions.explanation: 'Bangaarada Manushya' (1972) was one of Dr. Rajkumar's biggest hits, enjoying an extraordinarily long theatrical run.

questions.q: Who was Dr. Rajkumar's wife, who later became a prominent Kannada film producer?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar's wife Parvathamma Rajkumar produced many films under the family's banner.

questions.q: Which of Dr. Rajkumar's sons is a leading Kannada actor known as 'Shivanna'?
questions.explanation: Shivarajkumar, Dr. Rajkumar's eldest son, is a leading Kannada actor affectionately called 'Shivanna'.

questions.q: In which 1975 historical film did Dr. Rajkumar portray the prince Mayuravarma?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar played the Kadamba prince Mayuravarma in the 1975 film 'Mayura'.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Dr. Rajkumar born?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar was born in the village of Gajanur in present-day Karnataka.

questions.q: In which 1977 mythological film did Dr. Rajkumar play the dual roles of Arjuna and his son?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar played the dual roles of Arjuna and his son Babruvahana in the 1977 film 'Babruvahana'.

questions.q: Dr. Rajkumar became an icon during a movement championing which cause in Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar was a strong supporter of the Kannada language and a symbol of Kannada pride.

questions.q: In the 1965 mythological hit 'Satya Harishchandra', Dr. Rajkumar played which legendary truthful king?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar played the truth-bound King Harishchandra in 'Satya Harishchandra' (1965).

questions.q: Which Dr. Rajkumar film, set against the theme of forest conservation, features him as a forest officer?
questions.explanation: In 'Gandhada Gudi' (1973), Dr. Rajkumar plays a forest officer in a story built around wildlife and forest conservation.

questions.q: What dramatic event involving Dr. Rajkumar made national headlines in the year 2000?
options: He was abducted by the bandit Veerappan
questions.explanation: In 2000 Dr. Rajkumar was kidnapped by the forest brigand Veerappan and held for over three months.

questions.q: For singing in which 1992 film did Dr. Rajkumar win the National Award for Best Male Playback Singer?
questions.explanation: He won the 1992 National Award for the song 'Naadamaya' from the film 'Jeevana Chaitra'.

questions.q: Dr. Rajkumar's youngest son, who also became a major Kannada star, was known by which name?
questions.explanation: Puneeth Rajkumar, Dr. Rajkumar's youngest son, became one of Kannada cinema's biggest stars.

questions.q: Throughout his long career, Dr. Rajkumar was notable for acting in films of which single language?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar acted almost exclusively in Kannada-language films throughout his career, a point of pride for Kannada audiences.

questions.q: Which Dr. Rajkumar film is celebrated for the evergreen Kannada song 'Aadisi Nodu Beelisi Nodu'?
questions.explanation: The popular philosophical song 'Aadisi Nodu Beelisi Nodu' is from Dr. Rajkumar's film 'Kasturi Nivasa'.

questions.q: Dr. Rajkumar began his early performing career with which type of travelling theatre troupe?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar started in the Gubbi Veeranna touring drama company before entering films.

questions.q: Which civilian honour did the Government of India confer on Dr. Rajkumar in 1983?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 1983.

questions.q: In which year did the Kannada star Dr. Rajkumar make his lead debut with 'Bedara Kannappa'?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar's lead debut in 'Bedara Kannappa' was released in 1954.

questions.q: Which long-running 1972 Dr. Rajkumar film carried a strong message about hard work and rural development?
questions.explanation: 'Bangaarada Manushya' (1972) inspired many viewers toward agriculture and is noted for its record-long theatrical run.
